column1: #   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   object and event classes and CAPEC (Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and
#   Classification) schema entities. OCSF Attack/Technique/SubTechnique mirror
#   the ATT&CK-aligned concepts that CAPEC also catalogues at the attack-pattern
#   and technique granularity. Vulnerability-related classes are linked to
#   CAPEC AttackPattern as the mechanism through which weaknesses are exploited.

column8: OCSF Attack wraps ATT&CK/ATLAS tactic+technique references; CAPEC AttackPattern is the foundational attack-pattern concept from which ATT&CK descends.
Direct 1:1 — OCSF Technique carries ATT&CK technique id/name; CAPEC Technique models the same concept.
OCSF SubTechnique is a specialisation of Technique; CAPEC AttackStep models an ordered step within an attack pattern — the closest structural analogue.
ATT&CK Tactics and CAPEC Categories both serve as grouping constructs for lower-level attack concepts; semantics differ but the organising role is equivalent.
OCSF Mitigation describes countermeasures; CAPEC AttackPattern includes mitigation guidance — the link is informational rather than structural.
A vulnerability is often the entry point for a CAPEC attack pattern; the relationship is causal rather than definitional.
A vulnerability finding event may reference an associated CAPEC attack pattern via the embedded attack object.
Malware implements or embodies specific attack patterns; related rather than equivalent.
